patch.value: Non-blocking background search index refresh on mutations

Biggest per-command latency source with a configured semantic stack: every create/update/close AWAITS refreshSearchArtifactsForMutation inline (registration-helpers.ts:769), which embeds the changed item via Ollama (~350ms) AND rewrites the whole local vector store. Measured pm create = 1.8-2.4s in the dev repo. CPU profile: persistLanceDbLocalTable 189ms + parseLanceDbSnapshot 97ms + ~350ms idle (Ollama HTTP) + ~200ms corpus TOON decode. Sentry confirms real-world embedding timeouts (PM-CLI-A/9) blocking mutations. Make write-time refresh non-blocking: spawn a detached refresh child (telemetry-flush pattern in core/telemetry/runtime.ts is precedent) or debounce, with the reindex lock preventing concurrent corruption. Mutation returns immediately; index catches up; existing vector_index_stale warning already covers the window. Goal: mutations feel instant even with semantic search configured.
pm create/update/close return without blocking on embedding; index refreshes in background; no store corruption under concurrent mutations
